entry.title: Driving Successful SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Adoption: The Power of Change Management and
entry.content:
Implementing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) isn't just about rolling out a new system—it's about fundamentally rethinking how HR operates and delivers value. Successful EC adoption hinges on embracing change management and transformation strategies that center around people, processes, and technology working in harmony.

Transforming HR: Beyond Automation

Employee Central represents more than a digitization tool—it’s a paradigm shift in the HR toolkit. It enables a move from reactive, administrative HR practices to a proactive, data-driven approach that empowers employees and managers at every level. This shift is crucial because technology is an enabler, not a crutch to continue outdated or inefficient processes.

Adopting EC offers an opportunity to:

  • Rethink HR workflows: Instead of replicating manual, paper-based processes in a digital form, it's time to challenge why those processes exist in the first place. EC allows organizations to redesign processes based on best practices, efficiency, and user experience.
  • Empower the workforce: EC puts power directly into the hands of employees and managers through self-service tools for tasks like time-off requests, position management, and accessing pay slips. This democratizes HR, reducing bottlenecks and fostering ownership at every level.
  • Standardize globally, customize locally: EC supports global process standardization while allowing for regional compliance and cultural nuances. This balance ensures HR processes are streamlined and consistent, while still respecting local requirements.

Key takeaway: Automating bad processes only digitizes inefficiency. The real power of EC lies in rethinking HR delivery, simplifying operations, and enhancing the employee experience.

 

Common Roadblocks to EC Adoption

 

While the technology offers immense potential, many organizations encounter recurring barriers that prevent full realization of EC’s value:

 

Lack of Vision: Without a clear, shared understanding of why EC was implemented and what success looks like, teams struggle to align efforts and sustain momentum.

Inadequate Executive Sponsorship: Change needs vocal and visible leadership. When executives fail to actively support EC adoption and provide adequate resources to operate, resistance grows and transformation fails.

Ineffective Configuration: Implementing EC to mimic legacy processes limits its potential. Without reimagining workflows, organizations risk underutilizing the system’s capabilities. Configuration needs to evolve with time to always be aligned with changing HR programmes and tactics.

Siloed Ownership Between HR and IT: EC requires HR to own the solution, with IT as a strategic partner. When IT remains the sole owner, HR engagement and user-centric enhancements often lag.

Low User Adoption: When employees and managers revert to old ways of working (e.g., bypassing self-service functions), the value of EC diminishes.

Security Concerns and Infrastructure Gaps: A lack of trust in cloud security or inadequate infrastructure (e.g., mobile access for frontline workers) can block adoption.

No Continuous Change Management: Treating change management as a one-time, go-live activity is a common misstep. Ongoing communication, training, and feedback loops are essential to reinforce adoption on a solution that is constantly changing. 

💡Action Tip: Identify your organization's top two barriers today and create targeted plans to address them, whether through stronger sponsorship, better training, or rethinking workflows.

 

Value Management: Aligning People Priorities with Employee Administration Goals

 

Value management is where strategy meets measurement. It is the glue that binds your HR strategy with the operational goals of Employee Central. It ensures that your organization isn’t just using EC, but is also realizing measurable business value from it.

Without value management, there’s a risk that your EC project becomes just another IT system—operating in the background without truly impacting HR’s strategic goals. With value management, every action taken in EC is purpose-driven, aligned with business priorities, and measured against clear outcomes.

 

 

Here’s how it works:

Clarify Your Business Objectives (The 'Why'). Before diving into system metrics, step back and align on your HR priorities:

  • Is it improving data accuracy to support workforce planning?
  • Are you aiming to increase employee engagement through better self-service experiences?
  • Is process efficiency or compliance a key focus

Translate Business Priorities into Employee Administration Goals (The 'How'). Once the big-picture goals are set, map them to specific EC functionalities:

  • Accurate employee records → Supports talent readiness and workforce planning.
  • Self-service adoption → Empowers employees and reduces HR admin workload.
  • Process automation → Frees HR to focus on strategic initiatives like DEI, leadership development, or culture building. 

Example:

HR Priority: Improve employee engagement.

EC Goal: Increase adoption of self-service tools.

Metric: 90% of employees accessing their personal data via EC.

 

Define Metrics and Key Performance Indicators (The 'What'). Metrics turn intentions into measurable targets. Some common EC value metrics include: 

  • Adoption Metrics: % of vacation requests submitted via EC; % of pay slips accessed through self-service.
  • Process Efficiency: Reduction in time taken for headcount reporting; decrease in manual data entry.
  • Data Quality: % of complete and accurate employee records; % of positions regularly updated.
  • User Experience: Employee Net Promoter Scores (eNPS); reduction in HR service desk queries.

Build a Value Scorecard (The 'Dashboard'). A value scorecard brings transparency and accountability. It links HR strategy, EC goals, metrics, and current performance in one place, making it easy to track progress and adjust as needed.

Example Alignment:

 

People PriorityEC GoalMetric
Improve Workforce ReadinessAccurate Position Data95% of positions updated quarterly
Enhance Employee EngagementIncrease Self-service Usage90% of payslips accessed via EC
Streamline HR ProcessesAutomate Administrative Tasks50% reduction in manual HR interventions

 

This approach ensures that every EC initiative ties back to a business outcome, reinforcing the system’s value across the organization.

Monitor, Analyze, and Act (The 'Continuous Improvement'). Value management isn’t “set it and forget it.” Regularly review your metrics:

  • Are adoption rates where they should be?
  • Is HR achieving process improvements?
  • Are data insights driving better decision-making?

When gaps appear, take corrective actions:

  • Low adoption? Introduce refresher training or incentives.
  • Inaccurate data? Review processes for updating and validating records.
  • Slow reporting? Optimize workflows and ensure proper system usage.

Remember: Adoption alone isn’t the end goal. True value comes when EC helps HR deliver business outcomes—whether that’s improving employee experience, enabling better decision-making, or streamlining operations.

 

Life After Go-Live: Building a Sustainment Model

 

While a successful go-live for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) is worth celebrating, the real value begins after the confetti settles. Life after go-live is where adoption must deepen, processes mature, and the system proves its impact. A strong sustainment model ensures that EC continues to evolve with your organization’s needs and remains aligned to HR strategy and business goals.

Here’s how to build a post-go-live ecosystem that supports ongoing transformation:

 

✅Create a Dedicated Sustainment Team

After go-live, EC needs a cross-functional team that owns both the operational and strategic aspects of the solution. This includes:

  • Key User (HR): Deeply understands the HR strategy and business needs. Acts as a liaison between HR stakeholders and system admins. Usually embedded within the HR function, with 30–50% of their time dedicated to EC.
  • Key Admin (Technical/IT): Responsible for configurations, managing permissions, release testing, and integrating with other systems. Often has full-time focus on EC.
  • HCM Solution Admin: Oversees the entire SAP SuccessFactors suite and coordinates across modules. Ensures that changes in one area (e.g., EC) don’t create conflicts in others.
  • Partner Support: Engaged on a regular cadence to provide expert-level guidance, troubleshoot issues, and align your environment with best practices and product evolution.

💡Best Practice: Assign clear roles and ensure these individuals receive continuous training and certifications (e.g., SAP SFX accreditations) to stay aligned with product updates.

 

✅Define Core Sustainment Processes 

Your sustainment model must go beyond "keeping the lights on." It should proactively drive continuous improvement and innovation.

Innovation Management:

Review and assess SAP’s bi-annual release notes. Use structured processes (e.g., impact assessments, sandbox testing) to determine what features to adopt and when. Update configuration as often as HR processes, programmes or strategies change.

Data Management:

Confirm data architecture, define data catalogue, build governance, and monitor data quality KPIs (e.g., % of completed employee profiles, % of active positions updated). Define a process for data audits, cleansing, and integrity checks.

User Support and Enablement:

Provide tiered support, from help desks to admin support. Offer training refreshers, just-in-time guides, and contextual help in the system. Foster a self-service mindset.

Adoption Monitoring:

Track metrics like login frequency, transaction completion rates, and support ticket volume. Use these insights to spot bottlenecks and address low engagement areas.

💡Quick Tip: Treat sustainment like a product lifecycle—not a project. Establish a regular release calendar with internal “mini-go-lives” for new features.

 

✅ Establish Governance for Agile Decision-Making

Governance in the sustainment phase isn’t about bureaucracy—it’s about making the right decisions, quickly and collaboratively.

Set up a governance model with these levels:

  • Central EC Committee: HR Key User + Key Admin + Solution Admin. Owns day-to-day decisions (~60% of cases), such as adjusting workflows, managing permissions, or minor process changes.
  • Operational Committee: Includes HR leaders, IT architects, and PMO. Handles cross-functional changes that affect multiple processes or modules.
  • Executive Steering Group: CHRO, CIO, or transformation leads. Engaged for strategic shifts (e.g., expanding EC globally, adopting new modules, redefining core HR processes).

💡Use guiding principles (e.g., “adopt standard over customize,” “data quality over quantity”) to evaluate change requests and avoid solution drift.

 

 ✅ Embed Change Management as a Continuous Cycle 

Change management doesn’t stop at go-live. The sustainment phase must include: 

  • Continuous Sensitization: invite collaborators to be part of the cloud journey changing mindsets and with it - cultures
  • Ongoing Communication: Regularly share what’s changing in EC, what’s new, and why it matters. Highlight success stories and new capabilities.
  • Refresher Training: Offer quarterly training for managers, HR, and new employees. Use short, role-based modules to keep it relevant.
  • Feedback Loops: Collect input through pulse surveys, focus groups, or adoption dashboards. Feed insights back into improvement cycles.

💡Sustainment = reinforcement + optimization + user experience. The more EC becomes part of the “everyday fabric” of work, the more value you’ll extract from it.

 

 

✅Plan for Scalability and Continuous Growth 

A mature sustainment model anticipates what’s next:

  • New Releases & Features: Be ready to evaluate, test, and roll out new innovations (e.g., AI-based recommendations, new mobile capabilities).
  • Expansion into New Modules: Use EC’s strong foundation to expand into areas like Time Tracking, Performance, or Compensation.
  • Evolving Business Needs: Align system changes with shifts in HR strategy (e.g., new operating models, M&A, remote work, compliance changes).

💡 Run annual reviews to re-align your EC strategy with business goals. Consider a "year in review" dashboard to showcase achievements and justify future investments.

 

A sustainment model is not a luxury—it’s your safety net and your springboard. It ensures that SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central doesn’t just work—it thrives, evolves, and continually delivers value across your HR landscape.

 

By treating post-go-live as a long-term transformation journey, organizations can truly unlock the strategic benefits of EC—empowering employees, enabling HR agility, and accelerating business outcomes.

 

This blog post is part of the Path to Success series aimed at supporting existing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central customers adopt and consume more of the features and functions to achieve desired business outcomes.

The original webinar & slides are available on-demand here: link to the recording

We are committed to support all of our SAP SuccessFactors customers on their individual Path to Success. These sessions are part of a wide range of ways we can support your evolution to continuous value and driving business outcomes.  

 

#pathtosuccess #employeecentral #changemanagement

entry.title: Re: LIKP-TM_WHSE_EXEC value is not getting updated with t...
entry.content: The update to LIKP-TM_WHSE_EXEC will happen after few minutes.  If you have checked immediately, it won't get updated. Also, if you are in the delivery, come out of that and execute once again.

entry.title: Re: ALV Subtotal
entry.content:
Possibilities to solve:

  1. Run SOS to detect ALV inconsistencies (search ALV SOS in the forum).
  2. Restart your program if you didn't do it.
  3. Empty the ALV buffer by running the program BALVBUFDEL.

entry.title: Re: Error : Executing CAP with SAP HANA Cloud project as MTA
entry.content:
@YOJAN 

Try adding `/odata/v4/serviceName` to the URL.

entry.title: CDS View vs CDS View Entity in Query Browser
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I am trying to find my way around the query browser that is built in to the Fiori Launhcpad.
We are searching for solutions to enable reporting via CDS Views within the Fiori Launchpad.
Within the Query browser I was able to find standard and custom CDS Views, even built a very simple CDS View myself and I could retrieve data which is superb.
My issue is that since CDS Views are considered depracated as of ABAP 7.57 (as far as I know), I am trying to implement CDS View entities for the same purpose.

I used the appropiate annotations:

@analytics.query: true
@Analytics.dataExtraction.enabled: true
@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION

The view entity is still not listed in the query browser.
Did I miss some additional annotations or is it simply not possible for a view entity to be applied as an analytical query?

Thank you for your feedback!

Kind regards,
Tamas

entry.title: Re: convert nested JSON to ABAP
entry.content:
The code has no important change, it doesn't solve, the comments are not relevant for ABAP ("DEC").
You must indicate "GenAI was used to help generate this content" as per the Rules Of Engagement (https://pages.community.sap.com/resources/rules-of-engagement😞
"If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content.""
Moderator alerted to add the GenAI statement.

entry.title: Re: How to READ Deleted PR from MD03 in SAP.
entry.content: What's the value of IM_VSTAT (Include MM61XVAL, L for delete/löschen)

entry.title: Re: SAP learning journey
entry.content: As a data analyst I’ve worked on projects involving accounts payable and receivable, but my main focus has been on controlling and analytics—helping to streamline reporting, performance tracking, and data integration between systems.

entry.title: Re: Career ladder in SAP
entry.content:
Certainly The career ladder in SAP can be quite dynamic and offers various growth opportunities depending on your interests, expertise, and the path you choose. Here's a general overview of the typical progression:

  1. SAP Consultant (Entry-Level)

Role: As an entry-level SAP consultant, you’ll work on implementing, configuring, and supporting SAP modules (e.g., SAP MM, SD, FICO, etc.) for clients. This phase involves understanding business processes, configuring SAP solutions, and possibly doing some testing and troubleshooting.

Skills to Develop: Deep knowledge of specific SAP modules, functional understanding of business processes, and some familiarity with technical aspects (e.g., ABAP, SAP integration).

  1. Senior SAP Consultant (Mid-Level)

Role: After gaining experience as a consultant, you move into a senior consultant role. Here, you will take on more responsibility, leading smaller projects or teams, handling complex client requirements, and providing deeper insights into SAP functionality.

Skills to Develop: Advanced module expertise, leadership skills, and proficiency in project management. This stage may involve managing client relationships and overseeing the entire implementation cycle.

  1. SAP Lead Consultant / Project Manager

Role: At this stage, you typically move into a lead consultant or project manager role, where you’ll oversee larger SAP projects or manage teams of consultants. You'll handle multiple aspects of the project, from scoping and budgeting to managing timelines and ensuring the project is delivered successfully.

Skills to Develop: Strong project management and leadership skills, proficiency in SAP strategy, and experience in managing larger, cross-functional teams.

  1. SAP Solution Architect

Role: In this role, you would be responsible for designing the overall SAP architecture for the client, ensuring that the system is optimized to meet the business requirements. You'll work closely with business stakeholders and technical teams to ensure the architecture is scalable, secure, and aligned with the organization’s long-term goals.

Skills to Develop: Deep technical expertise in SAP as well as a strategic, holistic view of business needs. You'll need to be familiar with both functional and technical aspects of SAP.

  1. SAP Program Manager / Delivery Manager

Role: At this senior level, you’ll manage multiple projects or entire SAP programs within a company. You will be responsible for ensuring that the program aligns with business objectives, and you’ll be overseeing the implementation and operation of multiple SAP systems.

Skills to Develop: Leadership at an organizational level, risk management, and strong client relationship management skills. You’ll likely be involved in high-level decision-making and strategy for SAP initiatives.

  1. SAP Practice Lead / Head of SAP

Role: As a Practice Lead, you’ll oversee the SAP practice within a consultancy or large organization. You’ll manage teams of consultants, set the vision and strategy for SAP services, and ensure the practice is delivering value to clients. In larger organizations, this could involve overseeing the entire SAP business unit.

Skills to Develop: Extensive leadership, business development, and financial management skills. You’ll need to have a deep understanding of business strategy and be able to drive growth in the SAP practice.

  1. SAP Executive Roles (CIO, CTO, etc.)

Role: This is the pinnacle of the SAP career ladder. If you have strong leadership capabilities and a deep understanding of both technology and business, you could transition into executive roles like Chief Information Officer (CIO), Chief Technology Officer (CTO), or even Chief Operating Officer (COO). In these positions, you will oversee the entire IT or technology strategy for an organization, including SAP systems.

Skills to Develop: High-level strategic thinking, business acumen, and leadership at an enterprise-wide level. You’ll need to be comfortable making decisions that impact the entire company and shaping the future direction of the organization.

Summary of SAP Career Ladder:

Entry-Level: SAP Consultant

Mid-Level: Senior SAP Consultant

Higher-Level: SAP Lead Consultant / Project Manager

Specialized Role: SAP Solution Architect

Executive Level: SAP Program Manager / Delivery Manager

Leadership: SAP Practice Lead / Head of SAP

Top-Level: Executive roles like CIO, CTO, etc.

Where Does It End?

While the technical SAP career path can end at an executive level (CIO, CTO), many professionals move from technical SAP roles into broader management positions or even transition into other areas of IT leadership. Ultimately, your career path in SAP can be highly flexible, allowing you to either specialize further in SAP or move toward general management or strategic leadership roles.

The highest level typically involves executive leadership, but SAP experts can also pivot into other tech leadership roles based on the skills they've built over their careers.

The key is continuous learning, staying updated with SAP advancements, and strategically positioning yourself for higher-level roles as you gain experience.

entry.title: Re: What are the free certification available for Consult...
entry.content:
Hi @arymak87 
Sorry to hear that you are facing issues.
Can you please access this blog Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers and add your inquiry there.

Margit

a_agostini
 
 
 did you find the solution? We have same problems, component.js can't be loaded after upgrate to SPS08.
 
Seems that new upgrade changed some routing concepts.

entry.title: 'BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_SPLIT_DEC' after Partially picking
entry.content:
Hi, expert.
I've stuck with problem of using  'BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_SPLIT_DEC' after Partially picking.
I have a delivery with 4 items

I try to fill lt_itemdata table, with values, and i want just to save this picked quantity and transport the rest into new delivery.

And call FM

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_SPLIT_DEC'
      EXPORTING
        delivery              = vbeln
        businessprocess       = '0002'
        deliverynew           = newdelivery
        deliveryitemsbelongto = 'O'
        techncontrol          = techncontrol
      TABLES
        itemdata              = lt_itemdata
        return                = lt_return.

After executing i recieve the next : some items was saved with picking quantity, some was transport to new delivery with picking quantity.
OLD DELIVERY

 

NEW DELIVERY

 



Why it's happening?
How always to save the picking to old delivery?

This has become quite urgent now and noone seems to have a solution for this...

Thanks beforehand.

BR,

LM.

Warehouse Order and Task (A2X)

entry.title: Re: Hi, We are also facing the same issue. has anyone found the solution to this issue?
entry.content: With this post, nobody will be able to assist you. You have not shared what your issue is and what efforts you made to find out the root cause.

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information and User Assistance for SAP Digital Manufacturing, Release 2505
entry.content:
You can now access preliminary versions of the What's New information and user assistance for SAP Digital Manufacturing. These resources give you a comprehensive overview at the upcoming new and changed features in the release.

To find the preliminary user assistance, visit the Help Portal page of SAP Digital Manufacturing. For the Preliminary What's New information, use the What's New Viewer. As a customer or partner, you need an S-user to access both resources.

As you prepare for release 2505, we recommend reviewing the Manual Activities note on SAP for Me. In this release, we've also made extensive changes to Identity and Access Management. You can find detailed information on these changes in SAP Note 3585114.

entry.title: Version of PO changes and sequential print in PO form
entry.content:
We have a requirement to print the PO form and if any changes happen it should be version 1 and if again some change happen and the form is printed again it should print as version 2.

As of now it just prints Purchase order and in case the PO is changed it prints Changed Purchase order as text in the Header of the form.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Version of PO changes and sequential print in PO form
entry.content:
3351140 - Version Management for Purchase Order or RFQ - SAP for Me

 Unfortunately as per above KBA we don't have it currently for PO.

 However you may create a custom field and add the same to output form.

entry.title: Changing the export to CSV default delimiter from comma(,) to semicolon(;) in SAC
entry.content:
Hi SAC  Experts,

Currently, the export to CSV script uses a comma (,) as the default delimiter. To better accommodate German users, we can change the default delimiter to a semicolon (;). This adjustment ensures compatibility with regional preferences and enhances usability
Button to Export to CSV:

Script to export to CSV:

entry.title: Re: Is there a tutorial for getting started with SAP .NET...
entry.content:
I think, the following website should be helpful:

SAP Connector for Microsoft .NET

Here you will find a small "Overview" document (PDF), explaining the basic concepts, and also a zip file with C# and VB.NET code samples ("Tutorial.zip").

For the finer details, you can then consult the "Programming Guide" (also in PDF form).

entry.title: Re: How to READ Deleted PR from MD03 in SAP.
entry.content: It is IM_VSTAT = 'L'

entry.title: Re: getting "system" as value for "createdBy" aspect in sap capm
entry.content:
If you're making the post call from Postman using your auth instance's oauth credentials from it's security key; the $user or cds.User will always be 'system'.
And so, you will have to use an AppRouter.

Hemant

entry.title: Re: filter bars with Smart variant managements are not wo...
entry.content:
we found the answer to this question , in case some one facing the similar question, here the answer:
make sure the persistencyKey value are the same across the smart variant management and the filter bar. 
Then , when instantiate the PersonalizableInfo, make sure the keyName property is "persistencyKey".
This is to tell the control to look for the property name "persistencyKey" of the fitler bar and register it.

Hope it helps!

entry.title: Requirement to add 3 custom calculated fields in F1595 app
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have a requirement to add 3 custom calculated fields first is the date field which I have to select based on join conditions from standard table and other 2 fields are the calculated days fields which will show the remaining days based on date calculations. I have to add these fields using custom fields logic in the Standard Fiori application Stock-Multiple materials F1595. First I did not find any business context for this app, and I also did not able to find any BAdI for this app. So can you help me how to add these fields in this app and where to implement the logic to populate the data to those fields?

 

#SAP #Stock Multiple Material #F1595 #SAPFiori

entry.title: Asset acquisition posting in special period 13 Public Cloud leads to error F5567
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I would like to post an asset acquisition in period 13 in 2024.

I have created a new asset in the app “Manage Fixed Assets”, but I cannot post an acquisition to 31.12.2024.
I always get the following error message:

Unfortunately, I cannot select or enter a period in the asset posting.

The posting periods are open for the correction 2024 period 013 - 016 and from the normal periods of 2025 001 - 002.

Maybe you have an idea how to solve this. Or maybe it is not even possible to book asset accesses in the special periods in the public cloud?

Kind regards,

Vanessa

#F5567 #AssetAcquisition #period13

entry.title: Re: Visual Studio 2019 B1 Studio compatibility
entry.content: ...correction: Maybe B1-Studio in FP2502 has become more restrictive when modifing OnInitializeComponent(): I've removed the //Comments and it's no moore destroyed...

entry.title: Re: Determining the " Task Code Text " for a notification in QM02 ??
entry.content: You can use QPCT-KURZTEXT field for TASK CODE TEXT

entry.title: Re: Need CDS view for customer Project Actual cost and Bi...
entry.content:
Hi Chuma,

Thank you for your feedback and suggestions.

I forgot to mention that I'm using "Project ID" as the primary field.

The suggested CDS views don't have the "Project ID" field. Is there any other way to retrieve the information using the "Project ID" field?

Thanks & Regards,
Vignesh T

entry.title: Unable to Customize Production Order / Production Task Form Template via Easy Form Editor
entry.content:
You have tried several times to modify the operation table/ columns in the production order or production task form template via Easy Form Editor, however they are not saved.

Refresh the page and re-open the Form Template and notice the modification is not reflected in Form Template.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Customize Production Order / Production Tas...
entry.content: This is a known limitation of the Production Order/Production Task Form Template. These forms cannot be modified using the Easy Form Editor. Please use Adobe LiveCycle Designer (ALD) to customize the forms.

entry.title: 🚀 SAP AI QuickLaunch Series / 秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Agent 開発 🚀 - Part3 Agent Tools / Agentツール
entry.content:
Generative AI has leapt from research papers to daily business reality— and SAP is surfing that wave at full speed. In this hands‑on series, I’ll show you how to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes, then grow it into a production‑ready asset—without drowning in theory.

生成 AI は研究室の外へ飛び出し、今やビジネス現場の常識を塗り替えています。SAPは全速力でその波に乗っています。このブログシリーズでは、SAP AI Core の既定モデルを最速で呼び出し、実務で使える AI エージェントへ拡張する“秒速ハンズオン”をお届けします。

 

📖What You’ll Learn in This Series / 本シリーズで学べること

  • How to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes
    SAP AI Core 上でカスタム AI エージェントを “秒速” で動かす方法

  • Hands‑on with LangChain, Google Search Tool, RAG, and Streamlit
    LangChain・Google 検索ツール・RAG・Streamlit を使った実装

  • Exposing the agent as a REST API and rebuilding the UI in SAPUI5/Fiori
    エージェントを REST API 化し、UI を SAPUI5/Fiori に載せ替える手順

Time Commitment / 学習時間
Each part is designed to be completed in 10–15 minutes.
各章は 10–15 分 で読める＆手を動かせるを予定しています。

 

🗺️ Series Roadmap / 連載ロードマップ

  • Part 0 Prologue / プロローグ
  • Part 1 Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad / 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad
  • Part 2 Building a Chat Model with LangChain / LangChain でチャットモデルを構築
  • Part 3 Agent Tools: Integrating Google Search / Agentツール: Google 検索を統合 [current blog / 現在のブログ]
  • Part 4 RAG Basics ①: File Upload & HANA Cloud VectorEngine / RAG 基礎 ①: ファイルアップロード & HANA Cloud ベクターエンジン
  • Part 5 RAG Basics ②: Retriever Tool / RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築
  • Part 6 Streamlit UI Prototype / Streamlit でチャットUI
  • Part 7 Expose as a REST API / AI Agent を REST API 化
  • Part 8 Rebuild the UI with SAPUI5 / SAPUI5 でチャットUI を再構築

Note / 注記
Subsequent blogs will be published soon.
続編は順次公開予定です。

Agent Tools: Integrating Google Search
Agentツール: Google 検索を統合

1 | Overview / 概要

An AI agent is more than a chat model. It follows the ReAct paradigm—short for Reason + Act—proposed by Yao et al. in 2022. In ReAct, an LLM writes out a reasoning trace and issues concrete actions (tool calls) in the same turn. Those actions can query Google, look up a database, or call any function you give it. The newly‑fetched data is fed back into the model so the next thought is grounded in fresh facts, reducing hallucinations.

AI Agent は単なるチャットモデルではありません。2022 年に Yao らが提唱した ReAct（Reason + Act）パラダイム に従い、LLM は 推論の過程 と 具体的な行動（ツール呼び出し） を同じターンで生成します。行動として Google 検索や DB 参照などを実行し、その結果を改めて LLM に渡すことで次の思考が最新情報に裏付けられ、ハルシネーションを抑えられます。

LangChain the “ReAct agent” pattern is already built‑in. We just register tools (like google_search) and LangChain decides, at runtime, whether the model should think or act.

LangChain には “ReAct Agent” パターンが標準搭載されています。google_search などのツールを登録するだけで、実行時にモデルが「思考すべきか／行動すべきか」を自律的に判断します。

 

2 | Prerequisites / 事前準備

  • BTP sub-account / BTP サブアカウント
  • SAP AI Core instance / SAP AI Core インスタンス
  • SAP AI LaunchPad Subscription / SAP AI Launchpadのサブスクリプション
  • Python 3.13 and pip / Python 3.13環境 & pip
  • VSCode, BAS or any IDE / VSCodeやBASなどのIDE

 

3 | Step 1 – Get a Google API Key / Google API キーを取得する

LangChain needs your Google API key and Custom Search Engine (CSE) ID so the agent can legally query Google. Think of it as giving your assistant a badge to enter the web.

Official docs : LangChain already provides a ready‑made Google Search Tool page.

公式ドキュメント : LangChain の Google Search Tool 解説ページ

  1. Open the Google Cloud Console. / Google Cloud コンソールを開きます。
  2. Select or create a project, and click “Create credentials → API key”. A dialog shows your new key.
    プロジェクトを選択または新規作成して 「認証情報を作成 → API キー」 をクリックします。ダイアログに新しいキーが表示されます。

    Tip: rename the key to something memorable. / 後で識別しやすいようにキー名を変更しておくと便利です。
  3.  Go to the Programmable Search Engine create page. / Programmable Search Engine の作成画面へ移動します。
  4. Enter any name, set Search the entire web, and click “Create.”
    任意の検索エンジン名を入力し、検索対象を「ウェブ全体」に設定して 「作成」 をクリックします。
  5.  A screen with an HTML snippet appears.
    すると下記のような HTML スニペットと管理画面が表示されます。
<script async src="https://cse.google.com/cse.js?cx=GOOGLE_CSE_ID">
</script>
<div class="gcse-search"></div>​

  • Add both values to your project‑root .env file and keep them out of source code.
    2 つの値をプロジェクト直下の .env に追記し、ソースコードへベタ書きしないようにしましょう。
GOOGLE_CSE_ID = "your GOOGLE_CSE_ID"
GOOGLE_API_KEY = "your GOOGLE_API_KEY"​


Your notebook can now authenticate to Google when the agent calls google_search!

Agentが google_search ツールを呼び出す際に Google へ認証できる準備が整いました！

 

4 | Step 2 – Add the Google Search Tool to LangChain / Google検索ツールを実装する

We wrap Google’s JSON API inside a LangChain Tool so the agent can call google_search the same way it calls the LLM.

Google の JSON API を LangChain Tool にラップし、エージェントが LLM と同じ感覚で google_search を呼べるようにします。

Open the notebook from Part 2, and add the following cell.

Part 2 のノートブックを開きます。そして、次のセルを追加します。

# ▶ Notebook Cell 3
from langchain.tools import Tool
from langchain_community.utilities import GoogleSearchAPIWrapper

search = GoogleSearchAPIWrapper(k=5) # return top‑5 results

google_tool = Tool.from_function(
    name="google_search",
    description="Search Google and return the first results",
    func=search.run
)

What exactly happens in this step? / このステップで何をしているか？

  • GoogleSearchAPIWrapper: A class that returns a search result string based on the query entered by the agent.
    GoogleSearchAPIWrapper: Agent が入力した検索クエリに応じて、検索結果の文字列を返すクラスです。

  • Tool.from_function: converts any Python function into a LangChain Tool by attaching a human‑readable name and description.
    Tool.from_function: 任意の Python 関数に name と description を付けて LangChain Tool 化します。

  • description: Agents refer to the description of each tool to determine when and how to use it.
    Think of it as an “instruction manual” for the model.
    description: Agent は各ツールの説明文を読むことで、「いつ・どのようにツールを使うか」を判断します。これは、モデルにとっての“取扱説明書”のような役割を果たします。

 

5 | Step 3 – Build a LangChain Agent / LangChain エージェントを構築

We combine the chat model from Part 2 with the Google tool to form an Agent that can decide on‑the‑fly whether it needs external information.

Part 2 で作った チャットモデル と Google ツール を合体させ、必要に応じて外部情報を取りに行く Agent を作成します。

# ▶ Notebook Cell 4
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType

agent = initialize_agent(
    tools=[google_tool],
    llm=chat_llm,
    agent=AgentType.OPENAI_FUNCTIONS,
    verbose=True,
)

agent.invoke("Who won the Tokyo Marathon in 2025, and what was the finishing time?")

What exactly happens in this step? / このステップで何をしているか？

  • initialize_agent: wires your LLM, tools, and optional memory into a single Agent object.
    initialize_agent: LLM・ツール群・メモリをまとめて 1 つの Agent に組み上げます。
  • tools=[google_tool]: hands the agent a toolbox—right now it contains only Google Search, but you can append more later.
    tools=[google_tool]: Agentにツールボックスを渡します。現時点では Google Search だけですが、後で追加可能です。
  • agent=AgentType.OPENAI_FUNCTIONS: Since we’re using OpenAI’s LLM in this case, we’ll choose the function calling specification that is compatible with OpenAI models.
    今回は OpenAI の LLM を使用するため、OpenAI モデルに対応したツール呼び出し（Function Calling）の仕様を選択します。

Expect to see a printed google_search call, followed by the champion’s name and time!

google_search 呼び出しログとともに、優勝者とタイムが表示されれば OK です！

 

6 | Challenge – Add the original Tool! / チャレンジ – オリジナルツールを追加しよう

Let’s create an agent that draws omikuji (a Japanese fortune) while following LangChain’s “How to create tools” guide.

LangChain の「ツールの作り方（How to create tools）」を参考にしながら、おみくじを引く Agent を作ってみましょう。

# ▶ Notebook Cell 5
import random

def omikuji(_: str) -> str:
    return random.choice([
        "XXXXXX"
    ])

omikuji_tool = Tool.from_function(
    name="xxxxxx",
    description="......",
    # ?????
)

omikuji_agent = initialize_agent(
    tools=XXXXXXXX,
    llm=chat_llm,
    agent=AgentType.OPENAI_FUNCTIONS,
    verbose=True,
)

omikuji_agent.invoke("おみくじを引いてください")

If agent.invoke("おみくじを引いてください") returns one of the predefined results, it's a pass!

agent.invoke("おみくじを引いてください") が、指定した結果のいずれかを返せば合格です！

Have fun customizing! / 自分だけのツールをどんどん追加して遊んでみましょう。

 
 

7 | Next Up / 次回予告

Part 4 RAG Basics ①: File Upload & HANA Cloud VectorEngine / RAG 基礎 ①: ファイルアップロード & HANA Cloud ベクターエンジン

In Part 4 we upload PDFs, vectorise them in HANA Cloud Vector Engine, and let the agent answer with your own documents — the first step toward full‑blown RAG.

Part 4 では PDF をアップロードし HANA Cloud Vector Engine にベクトル化。エージェントが自社文書を引用して回答する RAG の第一歩に進みます。

 

Disclaimer / 免責事項

Disclaimer – All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.
免責事項 – 本ブログに記載された見解および意見はすべて私個人のものであり、私の個人的な立場で発信しています。SAP は本ブログの内容について一切の責任を負いません。

entry.title: Re: Need CDS view for customer Project Actual cost and Billed & Unbilled information in SAP Publ
entry.content: Hi Chuma, Thanks for your feedbacks and suggestions I missed to say that , I'm using "Project ID" as a prime field suggested CDS views dnt have the "Project ID" field, Is there any other ways to get the information with "project ID" field??

entry.title: SAC to Datasphere
entry.content:
hi community,
Q1.) Can anyone guide me that how to create "Planning Model" in Datasphere ?

Q2.) Is this possible in SAC that a email notification generate after a condition meet ?

entry.title: How can we set the credit limit for facility product type 56A?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I created a credit facility using product type 56A & transaction type 200. I have flow types 1204, 1205,1206 & 1207 associated to the facility. I created a total credit line of 10 million dollars in the profiles tab & created condition types 1204 & 1205 in charges tab. bilateral facility assigned successfully and when i am trying to obtain facility 12million but didn't display any error so how can be defined credit limit for facility. Please advise.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Integrate Learning with Development Goals
entry.content: Hi there, Actually this is not true for Classes. The Configuration of the Integration between Development Plan and Learning only allows you to add an item to the learning plan of the user. So if the Approval Process is in a class and not the item directly there is no way to trigger an e-mail for approval related notifications.

entry.title: CDS views for Manage Workforce app SAP Public Cloud 2502
entry.content:
Hi,

In order to export employee master, we have to refer to multiple CDS views. Infact, I checked multiple CDS views in Customer Data Browser app but email id is missing in all of them. I need to know if there is any standard CDS view which contain all the fields including email id of the employee. 

Downloading multiple CDS files and then compiling is becoming a task now. Please help.

entry.title: Re: Fiori OVP Analytical Card - Error OData Property 'NETPRICEAMOUNTAGGREGATE' already exists
entry.content:
I have tried some other things like adding and removing the annotations @Aggregation.default: #SUM.

But it does not matter it seems the OData V4 Interpreter has an error. Because the Data Points work:

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
Hi, 

You mention that there will be a new Horizon UI in ECP

Will the Horizon UI replace the current SAP GUI ABAP transaction look and feel or how should I understand it? 

Please send some screenshots if you have any. 

@RajivNema 

Best regards, 

Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Process Navigator API
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thank you for your engagement in the SAP Signavio Community!

There is no API available for the SAP Signavio Process Navigator. However, you can use the Value Accelerator Library to install the complete solution scenario content in SAP Signavio.

Best regards,
Janina

entry.title: Re: Subscription failed for SAP build process automation
entry.content: same for me

entry.title: Webinar Entitlement Management: Preview for Scope Item “5SW – Subscription Management for Software"
entry.content:
Join us for this webinar on SAP Entitlement Management, offering a deep dive preview of the planned Scope Item "5SW – Subscription Management for Software" on Tuesday, May 13, 2025, at 5:00 PM CEST / 11:00 AM EST.

This one-hour session will explore the business value and key capabilities of the scope item template, planned for 2508, for managing software subscriptions, with support for flexible software license bundling for multiple applications, tenant types, license metrics, and application capabilities/features. Learn how SAP enables end-to-end integration from sales to fulfillment, billing, and revenue recognition. Discover how to achieve fully automated fulfillment, provisioning, usage measurement, decommissioning and billing — with seamless integration across SAP Entitlement Management, SAP Subscription Billing, S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (optional integration to S/HANA Cloud, private edition with BRIM) and third-party systems.

A live demo will showcase contract setup, automated provisioning, license and software usage tracking providing insights for customer success, billing and revenue recognition, as well as and dynamic scenarios like contract upgrade and contract replacement with a higher edition without tenant disruption, and decommissioning. We’ll also preview the future roadmap, featuring AI-driven insights and analytics.

Wrap up with an interactive Q&A — register now and review the detailed agenda below!

More information on EMS - see my Summary Blog on SAP Entitlement Management 

Customer Webinar SAP Entitlement Management:
Deep Dive Preview for Scope Item “5SW – Subscription Management for Software”

Date: Tuesday, May 13, 2025
Time: 5:00 PM Central European Summer Time / 11:00 AM EST
Duration: 1 hour

Agenda:

1. Introduction & Overview of Scope Item 5SW

  • Purpose & business value of Subscription Management for Software
  • Key capabilities: End-to-end integration and automation from sales to fulfillment/provisioning, billing, and revenue recognition

2. Key Features & Capabilities

  • Predefined Integration Content
    • Software subscription modeling, sales, and automated fulfillment/provisioning
  • Automated Lifecycle Management in SAP Entitlement Management (EMS)
    • Provisioning, upgrades, decommissioning of applications, tenants, license metrics, and capabilities/features
  • Flexible Licensing & Tenant Management
    • Support for multiple applications, tenant types, license metrics, and capabilities/features
    • License editions & add-ons (upselling/downgrading without tenant disruption)
  • End-to-End Integration
    • SAP Entitlement Management + SAP Subscription Billing + S/4HANA Cloud, public edition
    • Optional integration with S/4HANA Cloud, private edition with BRIM & other contract systems
  • Revenue Recognition & Split
    • Handling revenue recognition and revenue allocation across multiple product areas

3. Live Demo: Subscription Management in Action

  • Contract Creation & Management
    • New subscription / extension in S/4HANA Cloud Solution Order
    • Subscription contract setup in SAP Subscription Billing
  • Entitlement & Provisioning Workflow
    • Automated tenant, license metric, and capability provisioning in SAP Entitlement Management (EMS)
    • Record license consumption, software usage and adoption - basis for data driven insights
  • Billing & Revenue Recognition
    • Handling one-time, recurring, and usage-based billing models
    • Revenue recognition and revenue split
  • Change & Upgrade Scenarios
    • Scaling users, extending duration, adding test tenants, trial licenses
    • Contract replacement (e.g., upgrading to a higher software edition)
  • Decommissioning Process
    • Handling end-of-subscription for licenses, capabilities, and tenants

4. Future Roadmap & Innovation

  • Enhancing Customer Success with Data-Driven Insights
    • Integrating software usage & adoption data to reduce churn & revenue leakage
  • AI, Analytics, and Integration
    • AI-powered agents & Insight Apps (leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Business AI)
    • Leverage SAP Business Data Cloud for integration to 3rd party customer success applications

5. Q&A & Next Steps

  • Open discussion & feedback

Hi

Please report support ticket using component SBO-BC-SLD to investigate this issue further .

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

Roger

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Connector 2.18 is out!
entry.content:
Thanks for releasing this new version of SAP Cloud Connector, this Subaccount certificate refresh will reduce manual effort a lot. 🙂 

Is there any plan for 2.18.1 release?

entry.title: Re: wage type 1544 adj gpssa not adding in employee salar...
entry.content: Your report zhrdata is a Custom Report.  Look in it's code if the program is "hard coded" with a series of Wage Types to report on, or if those Wage Types are listed in a Custom Table that is read by the program.  If you don't have access to look at the program's code, ask an ABAP resource to do it for you.

entry.title: How can we use association in custom CDS entity in RAP?
entry.content:
I have a use case where I want to use association in the custom entity in RAP. The Association/Composition  is not with the child but it is pointing to the independent entity. Is there a way we can achieve this?

Basically I want to expose value help in custom entity (From some other entity) which the framework is not allowing me.

entry.title: Re: How to READ Deleted PR from MD03 in SAP.
entry.content:
'L' is loeschen (en delete) and you have your answer 😎

*---> Verarbeitungsstatus
CONSTANTS:
      vstath(1) TYPE c VALUE 'H',      "Hinzufuegen
      vstatv(1) TYPE c VALUE 'V',      "Veraendern
      vstatl(1) TYPE c VALUE 'L',      "Loeschen
      vstatn(1) TYPE c VALUE 'N',      "Neu aufloesen
      vstati(1) TYPE c VALUE 'I',      "Einfuegen
      vstata(1) TYPE c VALUE 'A',      "Anzeigen/Aktivieren
      vstatd(1) TYPE c VALUE 'D',      "Deaktivieren
      vstatt(1) TYPE c VALUE 'T',      "Teilabbau
      vstatk(1) TYPE c VALUE 'K',      "Komplettabbau/Korrigieren
      vstats(1) TYPE c VALUE 'S',      "Simulieren
      vstatc(1) TYPE c VALUE 'C',      "Aggregieren
      vstatr(1) TYPE c VALUE 'R',      "Rückmeldung - Reduzieren
      vstate(1) TYPE c VALUE 'E'.      "Endrückmeldung - Löschen

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
Hello Sandra_Rossi,

I finally recovered all my data.

3 enhancements to retrieve the BOM:
- 1 to put the sy-batch = X
- 1 to put the sy-batch = space back
- 1 to retrieve the BOM data in a Z table

3 enhancements to recover my little table:
- 1 to force the value 'KEPH'
- 1 to restore this value as it was
- 1 to retrieve the table values in a Z table

I don't know if it's very clean to do it this way, in any case I don't have any other solution.

I add that without your help it is impossible for me to get out of it so I thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping for nonrecurring pay components
entry.content: Hi @atlchan - how were you able to get these to pull through? Also mapped via Recruit to Hire mapping (similar to yours); however, in RNHD in Onboarding, the portlet remains closed and no values display for Non Recurring / Variable Pay. It is working as expected for Recurring Pay Component portlet. Any advise would be appreciated. Many thanks

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Daniel Mateo
entry.content:
Impresionante todo lo que has vivido gracias a tu trabajo con SAP.

Lo mío es más localizado 😜

Gracias por la nominación, aunque has puesto el listón muy alto.

Un abrazo.

entry.title: Re: Successfactors Employee Central Deep Validations
entry.content:
Hello

Is it possible to use deep validation/automatic validation for IBAN in print format (accepting espaces)?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: CDS View vs CDS View Entity in Query Browser
entry.content: can you share the code how your entity is defined? not just those header annotation.

entry.title: Re: CDS View vs CDS View Entity in Query Browser
entry.content:
Query CDS View Entity:

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Generic Custom Sales Order Data Entity'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #D,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
@Analytics.query: true
@Analytics.dataExtraction.enabled: true

@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION
 
@Consumption.valueHelpDefault.fetchValues: #AUTOMATICALLY_WHEN_DISPLAYED
@OData.publish: true

define view entity Z_SO_DATA_QUERY_ENTITY 
  with parameters
    @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition:                    // Annotation to link parameter with CDS view which will help to generate Text
    [ {
    entity: { name: 'Z_SALES_ORG_VAL_HELP',
          element: 'Vkorg'}
    }]
    p_vkorg     : vkorg,
    @EndUserText.label: 'From creation date'
    p_crea_from : datum,
    @EndUserText.label: 'To creation date'
    p_crea_to   : datum

  as select from Z_SALES_ORDER_DATA_CUBE (p_vkorg:$parameters.p_vkorg,
                                          p_crea_from:$parameters.p_crea_from,
                                          p_crea_to:$parameters.p_crea_to)
{
  key vbeln,
      auart,
      vkorg,
      erdat,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'waerk'
      netwr,
      waerk
}

Cube CDS View Entity:


@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Generic Custom Sales Order Data Cube'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
@Analytics.dataCategory: #CUBE
@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION

define view entity Z_SALES_ORDER_DATA_CUBE
  with parameters
    @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition:                    // Annotation to link parameter with CDS view which will help to generate Text
    [ {
    entity: { name: 'Z_SALES_ORG_VAL_HELP',
          element: 'Vkorg'}
    }]
    p_vkorg : vkorg,
    @EndUserText.label: 'From creation date'
    p_crea_from : datum,
    @EndUserText.label: 'To creation date'
    p_crea_to : datum
    
  as select from vbak as k
    join         vbap as p on k.vbeln = p.vbeln
{
  key k.vbeln      as vbeln,
      k.auart as auart,
      k.vkorg      as vkorg,
      k.erdat      as erdat,
      @Aggregation.default: #SUM
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'waerk'
      sum(p.netwr) as netwr, //Nettowert der Auftragsposition in Belegwahrung,
      p.waerk      as waerk
}
where
//      k.auart = $parameters.p_auart
//      and 
      k.vkorg = $parameters.p_vkorg
      and k.erdat between $parameters.p_crea_from and $parameters.p_crea_to
      
group by
  k.vkorg,
  k.vbeln,
  k.auart,
  k.erdat,
  p.waerk

entry.title: Stay informed about the latest developments and products - Register for upcoming live session
entry.content:
With your SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can take advantage of expert-led live sessions, to dive deeper into focus topics, ask questions and maximize your skills


SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Recipe Formulation in SAP Integrated Product Development:
    April 24, 16:00 - 17:30 (CET) or April 29, 09:00 - 10:30 (CET)
  • Get Certified Session: SAP Certified Associate - Backend Developer - SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
    April 29, 09:00 - 10:00 or from 16:00 - 17:00 (CET)

Artificial Intelligence (AI)

  • Managing AI models with SAP AI Core on BTP
    April 17, 08:00 - 09:00 (CET) or from 16:00 - 17:00 (CET)
  • Implementing real-world AI models on SAP BTP
    April 29, 08:00 - 09:30 (CET) or from 16:00 - 17:30 (CET)

Human Capital Management

  • Get Certified Session: Implementation Consultant - SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting: Recruiter Experience
    April 28, 16:00 - 17:00 (CET), April 30, 10:00 - 11:00 (CET)
  • Get Certified Session: Implementation Consultant - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core
    April 22, 09:00 - 10:00 (CET), April 28, 09:00 - 11:00 (CET)

As an SAP Learning Hub subscriber, you can join this live session to explore the various methods of data integration, with a focus on the best options for different scenarios. You will learn how to implement various data integration environments, ranging from a simple file import to an enterprise level data load. If you are involved in an incentive management implementation of any size, you don’t want to miss this session. Read more

Please note
You can find the full list of live sessions here and register directly with your SAP Learning Hub subscription.

entry.title: Re: First Half 2025 Release: What's New in SAP SuccessFactors Time Management
entry.content: Thank you for sharing this insightful post on SAP SuccessFactors Time Management updates for 1H 2025! Your detailed explanation and valuable perspectives are truly commendable.

entry.title: Re: Calling OData services in SAP Digital Mnaufacturing
entry.content: Hello, I am facing the same error, could you tell me how it was resolved? I am trying to connect DM to my S4/Hana and I am facing a 401 error

entry.title: Table ANLB-PERFY is incorrectly updated due to incorrect handling
entry.content:
Hello,

In reference to SAP note 1795678, i have an issue just as described:

Example for incorrect settings:

ANLB-PERFY = 365 and T090NA-XDAILY = initial

The solution 3 of the note states if table ANLB-PERFY is incorrectly updated due to incorrect handling it may need to be corrected with an ABAP program. Contact your SAP Consultant.

Does it imply a program, or direct change to the table is permitted since SAP does not provide a program to update ANLB table to correct the related table inconsistency ?

Any thought,

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Can we load data From Multiple Odata Queries to Singl...
entry.content:
Hi Nithin17 ,

I don't think we have that option in model level , if need to combine 2 Queries. we need to create 2 models and  do  Blending in Story level . 

Which i have given solution on New Model with import data.

Regards,

Amarnath.

entry.title: New Here
entry.content:
Thrilled to form part of the community. I am

super stoked to be adding SAP products as solutions for our client base 🙌🏻

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Daniel Mateo
entry.content: Great stuff, Daniel! I loved reading your post—your passion for SAP shines through, and that story in Bulgaria is wild! It’s inspiring to see how you’re combining your professional journey with your adventures.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud Intrastat Conversion of Regions
entry.content:
In SAP Public Cloud is there the option to define administrative regions specifically for Intrastat reporting? There is such an option for On-Premise, which allows to:

1. Define such regions, which are separate from the general regions in SAP.

2. Map these regions to the general regions. So that for example, if a product is sold to a destination in general region 22, in the Intrastat the region that is mapped to general region 22 is recorded.

I can't find the option for this within CBC for Public Cloud. Is the recommendation to set up entirely new general regions? What do we do with the existing ones, since those can't be deleted?

To give more context, we can't use the existing general regions, since they are defined with two-number codes, while the local legislation requires that the regions in the Intrastat report are only with three-letter codes.

Thanks,

Danail

entry.title: Email output completed in system but not sent from sap public cloud.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have done the setup for customer to receive invoice in output parameter determination and maintained email ID in customer master. Output status is completed at billing document header but its not sent from SAP public cloud system. We have checked in message email transmission app but there is no entry for the email ID.

Please advise how to resolve this

entry.title: Re: How to know if the element is an OData entity
entry.content: yes when working on Fiori apps OData is the our best friend

entry.title: Delta in DataFlows in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts 

 

I have a question , other than Replication Flows , Does Dataflows do delta ? 

 

1. CDS views 

2. Other databases 

 

regards 

Rishi

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud PCI DSS 2024 AOC?
entry.content:
Hello Simon,
I hope you're doing well. I'm writing to provide you with an update on the PCI DSS compliance status of SAP Commerce Cloud
The current PCI DSS v3.2.1 attestation for SAP Commerce Cloud is still valid. Even though the PCI DSS v3.2.1 standard was officially retired on March 31, 2024, the attestation does not expire. This means that if your organization's compliance requirements allow the use of an attestation under PCI DSS v3.2.1, there's no immediate concern and the existing documentation remains relevant.

However, if your organization's policies require certification under PCI DSS v4.0, I want to let you know that SAP Commerce Cloud is in the final stages of completing the PCI DSS v4.0.1 audit. We're awaiting approval from the auditor, which we expect to receive in the coming days.

It's worth noting that SAP Commerce Cloud, as a Service Provider, doesn't process, store, or transmit cardholder data. Therefore, individual customers are responsible for their own PCI compliance activities within their environments. This includes obtaining the necessary attestations and conducting periodic vulnerability scans as required.

You can request the PCI DSS AoC Report for SAP Commerce Cloud at the following link:SAP Commerce Cloud 2023 PCI DSS AoC Report

Once the new PCI DSS AoC Report is released, you'll be able to find it at the Compliance Document Finder.

For additional information, you can refer to the PCI Compliance - Help Portal.

entry.title: Re: How to attach file to Q&A and Blog Posts in Community...
entry.content: The file attachments will also display at the end of the post - but I guess you also already saw that 🙂

entry.title: Display the number of installments in the supplier item
entry.content:
Dear SAP Gurus,

Is there a standard way for the user to view the value of each installment of a given financing in the supplier's items or in some other application (FIORI)?

Example: $600.00 in 3 installments

$200.00 - installment 1/3 - due 04/25/2025
$200.00 - installment 2/3 - due 05/25/2025
$200.00 - installment 3/3 - due 06/25/2025

 

Regards,

Nali. Patricia

entry.title: My Admin User in Signavio is Locked and nobody can help me with unlocking!!
entry.content:
I have an Admin Signavio User which for some reason got locked. I have contacted the CIC several times and tried to raise Support Tickets to SAP without any luck. Can someone help me on this????

Thanks,

BR,

Evangelia

entry.title: Re: Error in Goal Plan when sum of weights is greater tha...
entry.content:
@Konstantina   under the goal plan xml inside <obj-plan-template> please update below code as per requirement then it will be a hard stop at goal plan level.

max-weight="100.0" min-weight="0" max-weight-per-obj="50.0" min-weight-per-obj="1.0"

 

Please mark this as answer so other can  get benefits from this information.

Thanks

Mridul

entry.title: Re: Error : Executing CAP with SAP HANA Cloud project as MTA
entry.content:
Thanks for the response

These two urls work from local BAS tool:
https://port5000-workspaces-ws-hqcms.us10.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/interaction_items/webapp/index.html
https://port5000-workspaces-ws-hqcms.us10.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/odata/v4/catalog/Interactions_Header

But these does not work from deployed url:
https://e54aba11trial-dev-democaphana.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/interaction_items/webapp/index.html
https://e54aba11trial-dev-democaphana.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com//v4/catalog/Interactions_Header

I do not even get prompted windows to enter login account --> authentication functionality.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Where is the dark theme?
entry.content: @htammen @Mandar_Damle @Tomas_Buryanek We are working on the dark theme and although we don't have the exact release date yet, it will happen sooner than later. We will get back to you with the release date as soon as we have it.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track 2025
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track 2025 Registrations Are Opening!

We are excited to invite you to SAP Inside Track Istanbul 2025 – the meeting point of the SAP community where knowledge and experiences are shared, and technical topics are explored in depth!

It’s a great opportunity to discover the latest SAP developments, gain new perspectives, and network with experts who generously share their insights.

Date: Saturday, May 10, 2025
Location: Yeditepe University, Faculty of Fine Arts, Conference Hall
İnönü, Kayışdağı Cd. No:326/A, 34755 Ataşehir/Istanbul

Registration: Opens on April 30, 2025, at 09:00 AM.

Seats are limited, so don’t forget to register quickly!

At this year’s event, you’ll gain valuable insights into the latest SAP trends and innovations and have the chance to hear from distinguished speakers.
Participation is completely free and open to everyone!

Let’s learn, grow, and strengthen our SAP community together.
Don’t forget to register and join us at this great gathering!

entry.title: Re: Random P-User selected during login to Community 2024 (I can't choose)

entry.title: Re: Search in Community does not consider the content in...
entry.content: Hi @Abhijit_Shinde - I have taken note of the request, and I will add it to our change request list. We are continuously working on improving search in the community.

entry.title: importing users data
entry.content: When importing new hires data, the system doesn"t show error message if a required field is not filled, is there a way to enforce importing data with all mandatory fields
